key_insight: A multi-task spatiotemporal deep learning model can jointly predict laser welding penetration state, penetration depth, and weld seam morphology from weld-pool images plus process parameters.
engineering_relevance: This enables in-situ quality monitoring and closed-loop control for laser penetration welding, helping reduce defects and improve weld consistency.
method: Uses CMOS camera top-view weld-pool images as the main sensing input.
Combines CNN-based spatial feature extraction with state-space modeling for temporal dynamics.
Trains a multi-task framework to predict penetration state, depth, and reconstructed cross-section morphology.
evidence_signal: On the test set, the model achieved 99.35% penetration-state accuracy, 1.79 mm penetration-depth error, and 95.65% cross-section reconstruction accuracy.
watch_item: Performance may depend on camera viewpoint, welding setup, and dataset construction quality, so generalization to new materials or process windows should be verified.
paper_title: A multi-task spatiotemporal deep neural network for predicting penetration depth and morphology in laser welding
